coaching.coaching: Plan how verification of AC completeness and testability will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: verification of AC completeness and testability

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 — e.g., an exhaustive pytest parametrize over all registered store-touching commands verifying each triggers the gate, or a static analysis step (grep/AST check) that confirms the gate call site precedes all dispatch paths.
evidence: AC2 text contains no reference to a test, script, or check
Other ACs at least mention 'regression test' or 'guard test', but AC2 has none
A structural guarantee requires either an exhaustive enumeration + test per command, or a static analysis step — neither is described
findings.finding: AC2 ('no store-touching command can skip it') states a structural invariant but provides no concrete proving command. There is no described test, grep, or enumeration that would mechanically verify coverage at review/CI time.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'The mount runs at a single central CLI gate covering the pure intercepts; no store-touching command can skip it.'
scenarios: An agent could implement the gate for verify-commit-ticket only and satisfy AC1, while AC2 remains unverified because no proving command exists.
CI stays green because no test covers the exhaustive structural claim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the centralized CLI mount gate design and the policy=none exemption contract (init, scratch, explain, pure no-store reads) in an ADR or AGENTS.md update so future contributors know the rule and how to add exemptions.
evidence: 'Commands that must NOT auto-mount (policy=none): init (is init), scratch (fs-only), explain (guides, no store), and pure no-store reads' — inline in Design, no doc artifact named
'The mount runs at a single central CLI gate' — no ADR or AGENTS.md update mentioned
AC5 only mentions 'docs/cli-reference regenerated if the CLI surface changed' — does not cover the architectural decision itself
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural rule (centralized CLI mount gate running before all intercepts and dispatch arms) and a maintained policy=none exemption contract (init, scratch, explain, pure no-store reads) with no mention of capturing this decision in an ADR, AGENTS.md, or design doc. Future contributors adding new commands need to know (a) that auto-mount now happens unconditionally at the gate, and (b) which exemptions exist and how to add one — without a doc artifact, this knowledge is only in the plan/PR history.
findings.location: Design section (policy=none list) and AC2 ('single central CLI gate')
scenarios: A developer adds a new command that should not auto-mount (e.g., a future 'status' or 'doctor' with no store dependency) and doesn't know to add it to the exemption list, causing unexpected auto-mount side effects in fresh worktrees.
A contributor modifies _cli/__init__.py and, not knowing about the central gate contract, adds a new intercept before the gate, reintroducing the skipped-mount bug.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR or AGENTS.md section: 'CLI Mount Gate: ensure_initialized(init_only=True) is called at one choke point in _cli/__init__.py before all intercepts and dispatch. Commands exempt from auto-mount (policy=none) are listed in _NOMOUNT_COMMANDS = {...}; add new exemptions there with a comment explaining why.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the specific command names that qualify as 'pure no-store reads' in the policy=none list so the gate boundary is fully specified without codebase investigation.
evidence: 'Commands that must NOT auto-mount (policy=none): init (is init), scratch (fs-only), explain (guides, no store), and pure no-store reads'
The phrase 'pure no-store reads' names a category but lists no specific commands, unlike init/scratch/explain which are individually named.
findings.finding: 'pure no-store reads' is listed in the policy=none exclusion list but never enumerated. An executing agent cannot determine which commands qualify without stopping to investigate the codebase, making the gate boundary ambiguous.
findings.location: Design section — 'Commands that must NOT auto-mount (policy=none): … and pure no-store reads'
scenarios: Agent must guess or investigate which commands fall in this category, risking over-gating (breaking a read command) or under-gating (missing a store-touching command).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'pure no-store reads' with the concrete command names, e.g., 'version, help' (or whichever commands read no store state).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For ACs 1, 3, and 4, add a concrete proving invocation (e.g., `pytest tests/test_cli_mount.py::test_verify_commit_ticket_fresh_worktree -v`) so done-ness is unambiguously checkable.
evidence: AC1: 'instead of ticket store not found — regression test' — no test path or command
AC3: 'guard test' — no test file, class, or invocation
AC4: 'REBAR_TRACKER_DIR override still short-circuits the gate (tests/embedding unaffected)' — 'unaffected' is not backed by a named test or command
findings.finding: ACs 1, 3, and 4 reference tests ('regression test', 'guard test') but give no concrete proving commands (no pytest path, no invocation pattern). An executor cannot confirm done-ness without inferring test location and naming.
scenarios: Agent writes a test in an unexpected location; reviewer cannot verify with a single command
CI might already have a test with a different name that is wrongly credited

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion (with guard test) covering the 'pure no-store reads' policy=none carveout so it is verifiable alongside the explain/init/scratch carveout.
evidence: Design: 'Commands that must NOT auto-mount (policy=none): init (is init), scratch (fs-only), explain (guides, no store), and pure no-store reads.'
AC3 only names 'explain (and init/scratch)' — 'pure no-store reads' has no corresponding criterion.
ABSENCE: no AC says 'pure no-store read commands are not force-mounted — guard test'
findings.finding: The Design names 'pure no-store reads' as a policy=none category (commands that must NOT auto-mount), but no Acceptance Criterion verifies this behavior, leaving it as an uncovered described deliverable.
findings.location: Design section, policy=none list: 'pure no-store reads'; Acceptance Criteria (all five ACs)
scenarios: An implementer adds the gate and gates 'pure no-store reads' incorrectly (or forgets to), but no test catches it because no AC requires it.
A reviewer cannot sign off the 'pure no-store reads' carveout because no AC verifies it.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC3 or add AC6: 'Pure no-store read commands (e.g. <name a concrete example>) are NOT force-mounted and continue to work with no store present — guard test.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the error-handling contract for gate mount failure: define what exception/exit-code/message the user sees when `_create_tracker` fails at the central gate (e.g., propagate a typed `StoreInitError` with a human-readable hint, never swallow or produce a traceback).
evidence: ABSENCE: No retry, graceful-degradation, or error-surface spec for the gate mount step.
Plan states mount is 'a no-op under REBAR_TRACKER_DIR override' but gives no failure mode for the non-override path when `_create_tracker` errors.
AC only tests the success path ('auto-mounts…and resolves the id') and the policy=none guard, not the mount-failure path.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new CLI gate that runs `_create_tracker` (symlink I/O) as a new failure point, but specifies no error handling for gate-level mount failures — e.g., main repo store absent in a non-worktree context, filesystem permission errors, or a broken/stale symlink. If the gate throws, the plan does not specify whether the error is surfaced with a clear message, whether it falls back, or whether it silently corrupts subsequent command execution.
findings.location: Design section — 'The MOUNT half of init…should run at ONE central CLI choke point'
scenarios: Developer runs `rebar verify-commit-ticket` in a standalone (non-worktree) repo where no main store exists; gate errors with an unspecified exception rather than a helpful message.
CI filesystem permissions prevent symlink creation; command exits with an opaque traceback instead of a clear 'store unavailable' message.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'If the gate mount fails (e.g., no main store found, permission error), the CLI exits with a clear, actionable error message (exit code 1) before dispatching any command — no unhandled exception traceback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm or add that `_create_tracker`'s symlink creation is atomic (e.g., uses `os.symlink` with `FileExistsError` silently ignored, or an atomic rename), so concurrent gate invocations in the same worktree are safe.
evidence: ABSENCE: No mention of atomicity or locking for symlink creation at the gate.

Plan says 'idempotent re-mount is cheap and non-fatal' but does not confirm the underlying `_create_tracker` uses atomic symlink semantics (e.g., `os.symlink` raises `FileExistsError` without a rename/swap guard).
findings.finding: The plan does not address concurrent invocations of store-touching commands in the same fresh worktree — two processes hitting the new gate simultaneously could both attempt `_create_tracker` (check-symlink-exists → create-symlink), risking a TOCTOU race where one process sees a partial or missing symlink after the other created it, or both fail on a 'symlink already exists' error.
scenarios: Parallel CI jobs share a worktree directory and both run `rebar verify-commit-ticket` at the same time; one gets `FileExistsError` from `os.symlink` and the gate propagates an unhandled exception.
A slow filesystem makes the window large enough that both processes observe 'symlink absent' before either creates it.
findings.suggested_fix: In the implementation note, add: 'symlink creation must tolerate a concurrent creator — catch `FileExistsError` and verify the existing symlink points to the expected target; if so, treat as success.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC specifying the user-facing error message and exit code when the central gate mount fails, ensuring it is more actionable than the original 'ticket store not found' error it replaces.
evidence: AC1 only covers the success scenario: 'auto-mounts…and resolves the id, instead of ticket store not found'.
AC3 covers the policy=none guard but not the mount-failure path.
ABSENCE: No AC or design note specifying the error message/exit code a user sees if the gate itself cannot mount the store.
findings.finding: The plan specifies the happy-path UX for the gate (auto-mount succeeds, command proceeds) and the policy=none UX (gate skipped, command proceeds), but does not specify the user-facing experience when auto-mount fails at the gate — no error message text, exit code, or fallback behavior is described for this new interaction point.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 and AC2; Design section — central CLI gate
scenarios: A developer runs any store-touching command (e.g., `rebar ls`) in an environment where auto-mount cannot succeed; they receive an opaque internal error rather than actionable guidance.
A new contributor sees an unfamiliar error from the gate and doesn't know whether to run `rebar init` or check their filesystem.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'If auto-mount at the gate fails, the CLI prints a human-readable message (e.g., "rebar: cannot mount ticket store — run `rebar init` or set REBAR_TRACKER_DIR") to stderr and exits with code 1.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (and corresponding test) for the gate's failure path: when auto-mount is attempted but cannot succeed (e.g., no main-repo store, missing tickets branch), the CLI emits a clear, actionable error message rather than an unhandled exception or the original opaque 'store not found'.
evidence: ACs cover: (1) successful auto-mount regression, (2) exempted-command guard, (3) REBAR_TRACKER_DIR override — but NOT the case where auto-mount is attempted and fails
"resolves the id, instead of 'ticket store not found'" — implies mount succeeds; no AC for what the user sees when it cannot succeed
Absence of any 'mount fails gracefully' or 'error message when main store is absent' acceptance criterion
findings.finding: The testing plan specifies no failure-path AC for the new auto-mount gate: when the gate runs but mount itself fails (no main-repo store to symlink to, missing tickets branch, permission error), there is no AC or test covering the error behavior for the user.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — no AC for mount-failure path
scenarios: User runs `rebar verify-commit-ticket` in a worktree whose main repo has no store yet (no .tickets branch, REBAR_TRACKER_DIR not set) — gate attempts mount, mount throws, user sees an unhandled exception or an opaque error instead of an actionable message
CI environment where the main repo store is absent; the new central gate triggers, fails, and the error surface is untested
findings.suggested_fix: Add one AC: "`rebar verify-commit-ticket` in a worktree where the main-repo store does not exist exits with a user-readable error (e.g., 'cannot auto-mount: no tickets store found in parent repo') — guard test." Wire a test that simulates mount failure and asserts on stderr/exit-code.

findings.finding: The plan introduces a central CLI gate to cover 'pure intercepts' and 'every store-touching command', but only names `verify-commit-ticket` as the target. At least five other store-touching pure intercepts in `_main_dispatch` have the same `os.path.isdir(tracker)` without mount bug and are unenumerated: `verify-identity`/`verify-authorship` (verify_authorship.py:518), `verify-opcert` (verify_opcert.py:161), `trusted-env` (reads store tip position via `_tip_position`), `remote-cert` (writes to store via `append_event`), and `enrich` (passes `tracker_dir()` to `cmd_enrich`). An implementer reading only AC1 could write the gate and test only for `verify-commit-ticket`, leaving the other intercepts still broken in fresh linked worktrees.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design section, list the full set of store-touching intercepts under the gate's scope. In the Acceptance Criteria, expand AC2's guard test to cover at least `verify-identity` and `verify-opcert` (the other CI merge-gate commands most likely to hit this in practice), or add an explicit AC for 'all store-touching intercepts auto-mount'.

findings.finding: The plan names `verify-commit-ticket` as the only failing pure intercept but there are at least four additional store-touching pure intercepts in the same `_main_dispatch` region (`verify-identity`/`verify-authorship`, `verify-opcert`, `trusted-env`, `remote-cert`). The plan's exemption list ('init, scratch, explain, and pure no-store reads') does not enumerate which intercepts qualify as 'pure no-store reads', leaving the implementer unable to verify AC2 ('no store-touching command can skip mount') without guessing the scope of the gate.
findings.location: Design section — 'Commands that must NOT auto-mount (policy=none): init, scratch, explain, and pure no-store reads' / AC2 — 'no store-touching command can skip it'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Design section such as: 'The following intercepts are ALSO store-touching and fall inside the gate: verify-identity, verify-authorship (alias), verify-opcert, trusted-env (reads store tip), remote-cert (writes SIGNATURE event). None are policy=none. The policy=none set is exactly: init, scratch, explain, grounding-info, bridge-probe, config, llm, prompt, criteria, jira-onboard, and any intercept whose entire logic is repo-independent.'

findings.finding: The Design section names 'pure no-store reads' as a policy=none category beyond the three named exemptions (init, scratch, explain), but never enumerates which commands qualify. An executor must decide which additional commands get the exemption without guidance.
findings.location: Design section — 'Commands that must NOT auto-mount (policy=none): init (is init), scratch (fs-only), explain (guides, no store), and pure no-store reads.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'and pure no-store reads' with an explicit parenthetical, e.g. '(none beyond these three — all other CLI commands touch the store)' or list the additional commands by name if they exist.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
